drugs.notes: 100mg once daily. Dose escalation to 140mg once daily allowed if no haematologic or cytogenetic response at starting dose. Available as 20mg, 50mg, 80mg, 100mg and 140mg tablets. Swallow whole, do not crush or cut. Take consistently at the same time each day with or without food.

dose_modifications.notes: Stop until neutrophils >1x10^9/L then resume at original starting dose of 100mg
Resume at reduced dose of 80mg once daily
Resume at reduced dose of 50mg once daily for newly diagnosed patients; discontinue for patients resistant or intolerant to prior therapy
Stop until platelets >=50x10^9/L then resume at original starting dose of 100mg

dose_modifications.condition: Platelets <50x10^9/L, 2nd occurrence with platelets <25x10^9/L and/or neutrophils <0.5 for >7 days (chronic phase)
dose_modifications.notes: Resume at reduced dose of 80mg once daily
Resume at reduced dose of 50mg once daily for newly diagnosed patients; discontinue for patients resistant or intolerant to prior therapy
May receive starting doses but use with caution, particularly during dose escalation
No dose reduction required; low renal excretion means clearance unlikely to be reduced

Interrupt dasatinib until asymptomatic or returned to baseline. If no improvement within ~1 week consider diuretics or corticosteroids. After 1st episode resume at same dose; after subsequent episode reduce by one dose level; after grade 3/4 reduce dose or switch to alternative TKI

special_warnings: BCR-ABL tyrosine kinase inhibitor — Philadelphia chromosome-positive CML chronic phase
ECG at baseline for patients with symptoms of cardiac or pulmonary disease risk factors — QTc monitoring required
Pleural effusion risk: monitor for dyspnoea and fluid accumulation
Pulmonary arterial hypertension (PAH) risk — monitor respiratory symptoms
Test all patients for hepatitis B virus (HBV) before initiating; discuss HBV-positive patients with HBV specialist before starting
FBC weekly for first 2 months then monthly thereafter
CYP3A4 inhibitor and substrate — check for drug interactions before prescribing concomitant medicines
NPSA/2008/RRR001 oral chemotherapy alert must be followed
Must be prescribed under supervision of oncologist/haematologist
Film-coated tablets must not be crushed or cut — risk of dermal exposure

drugs.notes: 140mg once daily. Dose escalation to 180mg once daily allowed in patients not achieving haematologic or cytogenetic response. Available as 20mg, 50mg, 80mg, 100mg and 140mg tablets. Swallow whole, do not crush or cut. Take consistently at the same time each day with or without food.

dose_modifications.condition: Neutrophils <0.5x10^9/L unrelated to leukaemia, 1st occurrence (accelerated phase)
dose_modifications.notes: Check if neutropenia is leukaemia-related (marrow aspirate/biopsy). If unrelated, stop until neutrophils >=1x10^9/L then resume at original starting dose of 140mg

dose_modifications.condition: Neutrophils <0.5x10^9/L unrelated to leukaemia, 2nd occurrence (accelerated phase)
dose_modifications.notes: Resume at reduced dose of 100mg once daily (second episode)

dose_modifications.condition: Neutrophils <0.5x10^9/L unrelated to leukaemia, 3rd occurrence (accelerated phase)
dose_modifications.notes: Resume at reduced dose of 80mg once daily (third episode)
Consider dose escalation to 180mg once daily if neutropenia is leukaemia-related

dose_modifications.condition: Platelets <10x10^9/L unrelated to leukaemia, 1st occurrence (accelerated phase)
dose_modifications.notes: Check if thrombocytopenia is leukaemia-related. If unrelated, stop until platelets >=20x10^9/L then resume at original starting dose of 140mg

dose_modifications.condition: Platelets <10x10^9/L unrelated to leukaemia, 2nd occurrence (accelerated phase)
dose_modifications.notes: Resume at reduced dose of 100mg once daily

dose_modifications.condition: Platelets <10x10^9/L unrelated to leukaemia, 3rd occurrence (accelerated phase)
dose_modifications.notes: Resume at reduced dose of 80mg once daily
Consider dose escalation to 180mg once daily if thrombocytopenia is leukaemia-related
May receive starting doses but use with caution, particularly during dose escalation
No dose reduction required; low renal excretion means clearance unlikely to be reduced

Interrupt dasatinib until asymptomatic or returned to baseline. If no improvement within ~1 week consider diuretics or corticosteroids. After 1st episode resume at same dose; after subsequent episode reduce by one dose level; after grade 3/4 reduce dose

special_warnings: BCR-ABL tyrosine kinase inhibitor — Philadelphia chromosome-positive CML accelerated phase
ECG at baseline for patients with cardiac or pulmonary disease risk factors — QTc monitoring required
Pleural effusion risk: monitor for dyspnoea and fluid accumulation
Pulmonary arterial hypertension (PAH) risk — monitor respiratory symptoms
Haematological toxicity occurs earlier and with increased frequency in accelerated phase CML
Test all patients for hepatitis B, C and HIV before initiating; discuss HBV-positive patients with HBV specialist
FBC and LFTs weekly for first 2 months then monthly thereafter
CYP3A4 inhibitor and substrate — check for drug interactions before prescribing concomitant medicines
NPSA/2008/RRR001 oral chemotherapy alert must be followed
Must be prescribed under supervision of a haematologist
Film-coated tablets must not be crushed or cut — risk of dermal exposure

drugs.notes: 120mg once daily. Dose may be increased to 200mg once daily if no composite complete remission after 4 weeks of treatment. Available as 40mg film-coated tablets. Swallow whole with water, do not break or crush. Take at about the same time each day with or without food. Continue until disease progression, unacceptable toxicity, cure, or HSCT.

dose_modifications.condition: Differentiation syndrome — severe signs/symptoms persisting >48 hours after corticosteroids
dose_modifications.notes: Interrupt gilteritinib. Administer corticosteroids and initiate haemodynamic monitoring at first suspicion. Resume at same dose when signs/symptoms improve to grade 2 or lower
Discontinue gilteritinib permanently
Interrupt gilteritinib. Resume at reduced dose (80mg or 120mg) when QTcF returns to within 30 msec of baseline or <=480 msec

dose_modifications.condition: QTcF increased by >30 msec on ECG on Day 8 of Cycle 1
dose_modifications.notes: Confirm with ECG on day 9. If confirmed, consider dose reduction to 80mg
Interrupt gilteritinib until pancreatitis resolved then resume at reduced dose (80mg or 120mg)

dose_modifications.condition: Other grade 3 or higher toxicity related to treatment
dose_modifications.notes: Interrupt gilteritinib until toxicity resolves or improves to grade 1 then resume at reduced dose (80mg or 120mg)

dose_modifications.condition: Hepatic impairment — mild (Child-Pugh A) or moderate (Child-Pugh B)
dose_modifications.notes: No dose adjustment required
Not recommended — safety and efficacy not evaluated in this population
No dose adjustment necessary
No clinical experience; use with caution
No dose reduction required for disease-related abnormal haematological counts

special_warnings: FLT3 inhibitor — only for patients with confirmed FLT3 mutation (ITD or TKD) by validated test
Differentiation syndrome risk — monitor closely; administer corticosteroids and haemodynamic monitoring if suspected
QT prolongation risk — ECG monitoring: baseline, Day 8 and Day 15 of Cycle 1, then prior to each of the next 3 subsequent monthly cycles
Creatine phosphokinase monitoring: baseline, Cycle 1 Day 15, then monthly
FBC, U&Es, LFTs (including magnesium) prior to each cycle
Posterior reversible encephalopathy syndrome (PRES) — discontinue if confirmed
Pancreatitis risk — interrupt if suspected
Gilteritinib interacts with azole antifungals — additional toxicity monitoring including ECG required
Negative pregnancy test required before starting in women of childbearing potential; barrier contraception recommended
Minor influence on driving and operating machinery — dizziness reported
Must be prescribed under supervision of consultant haematologist
NPSA/2008/RRR001 oral chemotherapy alert must be followed
Not for use with prior FLT3 inhibitor therapy (except sorafenib or midostaurin in 1st line)

drugs.notes: 10mg/m² IV bolus over 5 minutes on Day +3 post-transplant. Dose rounded to nearest 2.5mg (down if halfway). Common doses: 5mg, 7.5mg, 10mg, 12.5mg, 15mg. Administer at 1700hrs. Confirm bilirubin, creatinine and mucositis assessed and dose documented by transplant clinician before each dose. T-depleted allograft: reduced dose schedule (Day +3 only at 10mg/m², Days +6 and +11 at 5mg/m²).
5mg/m² IV bolus over 5 minutes on Days +6 and +11 post-transplant. T-depleted allograft: reduced dose compared to T-replete schedule. Dose rounded to nearest 2.5mg. Administer at 1700hrs.

50% dose
25% dose
Omit dose

25% dose
Omit dose

dose_modifications.condition: NCI-CTC grade 3 or more mucositis on Day +11
dose_modifications.notes: Day +11 dose may be reduced or omitted — discuss with transplant clinician

pre_medications: Calcium folinate 30mg IV bolus (precise dose 15mg/m²) every 6 hours for 4 doses starting 24 hours after each methotrexate dose (Days +4, +7, +12)
special_warnings: Post-HSCT GvHD prophylaxis — T-depleted allograft schedule with reduced/modified doses vs T-replete
Restricted to Wessex Blood and Marrow Transplant Unit only
Check calcium folinate is prescribed before each methotrexate dose
Assess bilirubin, creatinine and mucositis before each methotrexate dose — transplant clinician must document dose to be given
Mucositis monitoring: if NCI-CTC grade 3 or more on Day +11, reduce or omit that dose
Methotrexate is non-vesicant
Dose rounding to nearest 2.5mg (round down if halfway)

drugs.notes: 10mg/m² IV bolus over 5 minutes on Days +3, +6, and +11 post-transplant. T-replete allograft: full dose on all three days (compared to T-depleted which uses 5mg/m² on Days +6 and +11). Dose rounded to nearest 2.5mg (down if halfway). Common doses: 5mg, 7.5mg, 10mg, 12.5mg, 15mg. Administer at 1700hrs. Confirm bilirubin, creatinine and mucositis assessed before each dose.

pre_medications: Calcium folinate 30mg IV bolus (precise dose 15mg/m²) every 6 hours for 4 doses starting 24 hours after each methotrexate dose (Days +4, +7, +12)
special_warnings: Post-HSCT GvHD prophylaxis — T-replete allograft schedule: 10mg/m² on all three days (+3, +6, +11)

protocol_name: HSCT Ambulatory Conditioning — Melphalan + Bortezomib SC (Multiple Myeloma)

drugs.notes: 200mg/m² in 500ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 30 minutes on Day -1. Dose rounded down to nearest 10mg. Short 90-minute expiry — must be made locally for individual patient. Administer at 1130hrs. For CrCl 30-50ml/min reduce to 140mg/m²; CrCl <30ml/min not recommended (clinical decision required).

1.3mg/m² subcutaneous injection over 5 seconds. Day -1 dose to be given 8 hours after melphalan infusion. Day +5 second dose. Dose banded per national dose banding table (2.5). For neuropathy grade 1 with pain or grade 2: reduce to 1mg/m².

dose_modifications.notes: Full dose 200mg/m²
Reduce dose to 140mg/m²
High dose melphalan not recommended — clinical decision required
No information available; no dose changes recommended
100% dose
Consider initiating at 0.7mg/m²
100% dose
Clinical decision required

dose_modifications.condition: Bortezomib: NCI-CTC grade 1 neuropathy without pain or loss of function
dose_modifications.notes: Continue at full dose

dose_modifications.condition: Bortezomib: NCI-CTC grade 1 with pain or grade 2 neuropathy
dose_modifications.notes: Reduce dose to 1mg/m²
Confirm with transplant consultant before proceeding

pre_medications: Aprepitant 125mg oral once on Day -1 prior to melphalan; then 80mg oral once daily for 2 days (Day 0 and Day +1)
Dexamethasone 4mg oral each morning on same days as aprepitant (Day -1, Day 0, Day +1)
Ondansetron 8mg oral or IV twice daily for 10 days starting Day -1 evening then review
Metoclopramide 10mg oral or IV three times daily until nausea subsides
Furosemide 20mg IV bolus at 0930hrs on Day -1 (repeat at 1030hrs if urine output <500ml since 0900hrs)
IV hydration: sodium chloride 0.9% with potassium chloride 27mmol 1000ml evening before melphalan
IV hydration: 1000ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 90 minutes at 1000hrs on Day -1
IV hydration: 1000ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 120 minutes at 1200hrs (post-melphalan)
Chlorphenamine 10mg IV bolus pre stem cell infusion (Day 0)
Paracetamol 1000mg oral pre stem cell infusion (Day 0)
Ciprofloxacin 250mg oral twice daily from Day +5 whilst neutropenic
Fluconazole 100mg oral once daily from admission until neutropenia recovery
Aciclovir 400mg oral two to three times daily from admission continuing after discharge
G-CSF (filgrastim 30MU SC once daily from Day +7 or lenograstim 33.6MU SC once daily from Day +5) until neutrophils >0.5
Proton pump inhibitor gastric protection throughout admission
Nystatin 1ml oral four times daily until count recovery
Sodium chloride 0.9% oral rinse 10ml four times daily until count recovery
special_warnings: High-dose melphalan HSCT conditioning — ambulatory setting with bortezomib SC
Restricted to units carrying out autograft transplantation
Melphalan has a 90-minute expiry — must be made locally; adhere strictly to 1130hrs administration time
Pre-hydration and post-hydration mandatory with furosemide-guided urine output assessment before melphalan
Urine output must exceed 500ml since 0900hrs before melphalan infusion can proceed; if not achieved re-challenge with furosemide and reassess at 1100hrs
Bortezomib Day -1 must be given 8 hours after melphalan infusion
GFR measurement (Nuclear Medicine or creatinine clearance calculation) required prior to Day 1
FBC, LFTs, U&Es, bone profile, CRP, LDH, serum immunoglobulins and electrophoresis, serum free light chains prior to treatment
Confirm with transplant consultant if signs of disease relapse before proceeding
Peripheral neuropathy monitoring for bortezomib — dose reduce to 1mg/m² for grade 1 with pain or grade 2

protocol_name: HSCT Ambulatory Conditioning — High-Dose Melphalan 200mg/m² (Multiple Myeloma)

drugs.notes: 200mg/m² in 500ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 30 minutes on Day -1 at 1130hrs. Dose rounded down to nearest 10mg. 90-minute expiry — must be made locally. For CrCl 30-50ml/min: clinical decision (dose reduction considered); CrCl <30ml/min: reduce to 140mg/m². No dose change for hepatic impairment.

dose_modifications.notes: Full dose 200mg/m²
Clinical decision
Reduce to 140mg/m²

IV hydration: sodium chloride 0.9% with potassium chloride 27mmol 1000ml evening before melphalan (completed by 0930 day of infusion)
IV hydration: 1000ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 90 minutes at 1000hrs on Day -1

special_warnings: High-dose melphalan HSCT conditioning — ambulatory setting, single agent melphalan 200mg/m²
Restricted to units carrying out autograft transplantation

protocol_name: HSCT Conditioning — Alemtuzumab + Busulfan (10 doses) + Fludarabine + Methotrexate GvHD Prophylaxis (VUD)

drugs.notes: 10mg in 100ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 6 to 8 hours on Day -8 (test/initial dose). Premedication with chlorphenamine 10mg IV and paracetamol 1000mg oral 15-30 minutes before. If rigors occur, slow infusion and administer pethidine 12.5-25mg IV under doctor supervision. Non-vesicant.

20mg in 100ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 6 to 8 hours on Days -7 and -6. Total alemtuzumab 50mg (10mg + 20mg + 20mg) over 3 days. Premedication with chlorphenamine 10mg IV and paracetamol 1000mg oral 15-30 minutes before each dose. Non-vesicant.

0.8mg/kg every 6 hours for 10 doses total. Diluted to 0.5mg/ml in sodium chloride 0.9%, infused over 2 hours. Schedule: Day -8 at 1000hrs, 1600hrs, 2200hrs (3 doses); Day -7 at 0400hrs, 1000hrs, 1600hrs, 2200hrs (4 doses); Day -6 at 0400hrs, 1000hrs, 1600hrs (3 doses — finishing at 1600hrs Day -6). Dose rounded to nearest 3mg (down if halfway) due to 15-hour expiry and high toxicity. Obese patients: dose based on adjusted ideal body weight. Non-vesicant. Busulfan TDM monitoring required.

30mg/m² in 50ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 30 minutes on Days -7, -6, -5, -4, -3. 5 doses total. Doses rounded to nearest 2.5mg (down if halfway). National dose banding tables not applicable in transplant setting. Non-vesicant.

GvHD prophylaxis: 10mg/m² IV bolus over 5 minutes on Day +3. Administer at 1700hrs. Dose rounded to nearest 2.5mg (down if halfway). Confirm bilirubin, creatinine and mucositis checked and documented by transplant clinician before administration. Non-vesicant.

GvHD prophylaxis: 5mg/m² IV bolus over 5 minutes on Days +6 and +11. Administer at 1700hrs. Dose rounded to nearest 2.5mg. Ciclosporin prescribed separately on in-patient prescribing system as additional GvHD prophylaxis.

dose_modifications.notes: No studies done; dose adjustment is clinical decision but unlikely to require reduction
No studies conducted; no dose adjustment recommended in renal impairment
Caution in severe hepatic impairment; consider dose reduction in patients with raised liver enzymes
No dose adjustment recommended in renal impairment

Adjust towards 50% dose
Contraindicated
No dose modification recommended for hepatic dysfunction

dose_modifications.condition: Methotrexate: NCI-CTC grade 3 or more mucositis on Day +11
dose_modifications.notes: Day +11 dose may be reduced or omitted — discuss with transplant clinician

pre_medications: Chlorphenamine 10mg IV 15-30 minutes prior to each alemtuzumab infusion
Paracetamol 1000mg oral 15-30 minutes prior to each alemtuzumab infusion
Clonazepam 500 micrograms oral twice daily starting the night before busulfan; continue until 3 days after last busulfan dose (seizure prophylaxis)
Metoclopramide 10mg oral or IV three times daily
Ondansetron 8mg oral or IV twice daily
Calcium folinate 30mg IV bolus (precise dose 15mg/m²) every 6 hours for 4 doses starting 24 hours after each methotrexate dose (Days +4, +7, +12)
Ciclosporin oral or IV — GvHD prophylaxis prescribed separately on in-patient prescribing system
Nystatin 1ml oral four times daily
Sodium chloride 0.9% 10ml oral mouthwash four times daily
Antimicrobials per individual transplant schedule (gut decontamination, antifungal, antivirals, antibacterials)
Furosemide 20mg oral or IV four times daily when required for fluid overload
Gastric protection per local formulary
Pethidine 12.5-25mg IV under doctor supervision for alemtuzumab-induced rigors
special_warnings: Reduced intensity myeloablative conditioning for allogeneic HSCT with Volunteer Unrelated Donor (VUD)
Restricted to Wessex Blood and Marrow Transplant Unit only
Busulfan TDM (therapeutic drug monitoring) required — 10 doses given every 6 hours over Days -8 to -6
Busulfan dosing in obese patients based on adjusted ideal body weight per SPC
Busulfan dose rounded to nearest 3mg (down if halfway) due to 15-hour expiry and high toxicity
Clonazepam seizure prophylaxis must be prescribed before busulfan starts and continued for 3 days after last dose
Alemtuzumab infusion-related reactions: fever, hypotension, chills, rashes, anaphylaxis risk — chlorphenamine and paracetamol premedication mandatory
If rigors occur during alemtuzumab, slow infusion rate and administer pethidine 12.5-25mg IV
GvHD prophylaxis with both ciclosporin (prescribed on in-patient system) and methotrexate IV bolus (Days +3, +6, +11 — on ARIA)
Calcium folinate rescue after each methotrexate dose mandatory
Check bilirubin, creatinine and mucositis before each methotrexate dose
FBC, LFTs and U&Es prior to initiating treatment; GFR by Nuclear Medicine prior to Day 1; LFTs and creatinine prior to each methotrexate dose
Confirm with transplant consultant if signs of possible disease relapse before proceeding
Fludarabine contraindicated if CrCl <30ml/min
